method: Fetched each LOW-confidence issue body via GitHub REST (gh api /repos/magicpro97/tui-translator/issues/{n}). Extracted Acceptance criteria, Test cases, Outputs, and Inputs sections. Mapped Outputs/Inputs to concrete repo paths verified to exist (src/audio/**, src/providers/**, src/pipeline/**, src/config/**, src/tui/**, .github/workflows/**, packaging/windows/**, deny.toml, PRIVACY.md, tests/**). Promoted rows whose issue body provides explicit AI-actionable acceptance criteria from LOW to MEDIUM; flagged #366 as needs_human_clarification because its outputs are exclusively human-only (recruiting, scheduling, identifying named reviewers).

rows.title: verification: recruit and schedule named Layer 5 human reviewers

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #366; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Operational/recruiting issue. Acceptance from issue body: (1) identify at least two named reviewers, (2) confirm one is fluent in Japanese AND Vietnamese for L5-2, (3) confirm one is a non-developer for L5-5/L5-6, (4) schedule the real-hardware Zoom session(s), (5) link the resulting acceptance log path verification-evidence/<rc-tag>/acceptance-log.md. The issue body itself states 'Automation cannot complete this issue.' Unblocks human-only Layer 5 issues #115-#122 and epic #19.
after.human_clarification_reason: Issue body explicitly states 'Automation cannot complete this issue.' All five acceptance items require human identity, scheduling, and live-meeting outputs that an AI agent cannot satisfy. Needs a human operator to assign reviewers and produce the acceptance-log.

quotes: Identify at least two named reviewers.
Confirm one reviewer is fluent enough in Japanese and Vietnamese for L5-2.
Confirm one reviewer is a non-developer for L5-5/L5-6.
Link the resulting acceptance log path (`verification-evidence/<rc-tag>/acceptance-log.md`).
Automation cannot complete this issue. It exists to unblock human-only Layer 5 issues #115-#122 and epic #19.

rows.title: MACOS-01 — Spike: macOS capture API, TCC, CoreAudio/BlackHole, and ScreenCaptureKit decision

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #450; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Spike/decision issue. Acceptance from issue body: (1) confidence reaches 1.0 for starting MACOS-02; (2) confidence reaches 1.0 or a follow-up blocker is recorded for MACOS-03; (3) Soundflower and bundled-driver paths are explicitly rejected; (4) TCC remediation path is documented; (5) Opus review confirms the decision record is sufficient. Test outputs: (a) CoreAudio/cpal hello-capture emitting 16 kHz mono i16; (b) 60s BlackHole 2ch capture with sample-continuity measurement; (c) ScreenCaptureKit audio-only prototype or Obj-C/Swift trampoline; (d) permission-denied/TCC behavior from terminal launch; (e) first-sample and steady-state latency on at least one Apple Silicon host. Output is a decision record in issue comments + implementation prerequisites for MACOS-02 and MACOS-03; no production capture code shipped here. Out of scope: shipping production macOS capture code.

evidence.source: Issue #450 body — Acceptance criteria + Test cases + Outputs + Out of scope sections

rows.title: MACOS-02 — Implement macOS CoreAudio/BlackHole capture MVP

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #451; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A1 CoreAudio capture evidence exists; (2) MAC-A2 virtual-device classification evidence exists or is linked; (3) no Windows WASAPI behavior regresses; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: 60s BlackHole capture with sample-continuity >= 0.98 at 16 kHz mono i16; missing capture device surfaces plain-English error; disconnect/reconnect does not crash and is logged with recoverable state; `cargo test` passes on macos-13 and macos-14. Outputs: cfg(target_os = "macos") capture module preserving AudioChunk format; macOS device listing; BlackHole/named-device capture route; evidence under verification-evidence/macos/. Out of scope: ScreenCaptureKit (MACOS-03), bundling drivers. Depends on MACOS-01.

evidence.source: Issue #451 body — Acceptance criteria + Test cases + Outputs + Inputs sections

rows.title: MACOS-03 — Implement ScreenCaptureKit system-audio capture and permission preflight

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #452; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A4 TCC preflight evidence exists; (2) MAC-A12 ScreenCaptureKit capture evidence exists; (3) i18n/localization explicitly out of scope; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: permission denied exits with code 2 + remediation steps printed; 5-minute ScreenCaptureKit capture continuity >= 0.95; no silent failure when Screen Recording not approved; macOS 14 and macOS 15 behavior recorded. Outputs: capture_source = "system_audio" path; permission preflight check; plain-English remediation message; ScreenCaptureKit capture evidence. Depends on MACOS-01, MACOS-02.

evidence.source: Issue #452 body — Acceptance + Test cases + Outputs + Dependencies sections

rows.title: MACOS-04 — macOS virtual mic routing via BlackHole/Loopback without bundled drivers

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #453; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A2 and MAC-A3 evidence exist; (2) no driver binary is bundled or redistributed; (3) Windows VB-CABLE classification still passes; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: classify `BlackHole 2ch`, `Loopback Audio`, `VB-Cable` Mac names; tts_routing = "virtual_mic" resolves to expected route; tts_routing = "both" routes to speakers and virtual mic without duplicate decode; when BlackHole is installed a smoke test writes non-silent PCM to it. Outputs: macOS virtual-device patterns + enumeration/probe; route selection for virtual_mic and both; no-driver-bundling policy in issue evidence. Depends on MACOS-02.

evidence.source: Issue #453 body — Inputs cite src/audio/virtual_device.rs and src/pipeline/playback.rs; Acceptance + Test cases sections

rows.title: CTRL-01 — Real-time input/output volume and gain controls

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #454; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOL-A6 evidence exists; (2) runtime controls work without restart; (3) no silent fallback on invalid config; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: gain values clamp at min/max; 0.5 gain produces RMS ≈ 0.5x input RMS within ±2%; hotkey change effective ≤ 50 ms; gain transitions use short ramp/smoothing (no zipper noise); max gain does not clip above target limiter policy. Outputs: input/output gain settings; TUI gauges + hotkeys; watch-channel-based live updates; RMS evidence artifacts. Out of scope: OS-level mixer synchronization (unless separate spike approves).

evidence.source: Issue #454 body — Inputs cite capture pipeline, PlaybackService, AudioSink, config hot-reload, TUI key handling; Acceptance + Test cases sections

rows.title: CTRL-02 — Real-time TTS voice catalog and hot-swap

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #455; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOICE-A5 evidence exists; (2) voice selection is provider-aware; (3) no concurrent second voice is introduced; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: mocked Google TTS request contains selected voice name; voice swap affects next synthesis call; p95 voice-swap-to-first-audio ≤ 500 ms for next utterance; invalid voice surfaces visible user error; in-flight utterance finishes (not cut) unless policy changes. Outputs: VoiceSelection model; provider voice catalog/listing; TUI voice picker; hot-swap state update path. Depends on CTRL-03. Out of scope: streaming TTS interruption/crossfade.

evidence.source: Issue #455 body — Inputs cite GoogleTtsProvider, TtsProvider trait, config/TUI controls; Acceptance + Test cases sections

rows.title: CTRL-03 — Single active voice invariant across single and dual modes

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #456; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOICE-A8 and VOICE-A9 evidence exist; (2) no existing DM-06 policy regresses; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: property/state tests prove concurrently playing TTS streams ≤ 1; dual mode with primary slot A suppresses slot B audio; dual mode with primary slot B suppresses slot A audio; second playback service/mixer creation returns explicit error or is structurally impossible; voice swap mid-utterance preserves invariant and applies on next utterance. Outputs: single TTS playback owner or mixer; primary-slot policy for dual mode; invariant tests; explicit error/guard for attempted second playback owner. Out of scope: simultaneous bilingual spoken output (forbidden by roadmap).

evidence.source: Issue #456 body — Inputs explicitly cite tests/dm06_dual_tts_policy.rs, TtsSource config, dual-slot pipeline orchestrator, PlaybackService

rows.title: MODEL-01 — Local/remote backend selection contract for STT, MT, and TTS

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #457; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) provider contract tests pass; (2) privacy/security review finds no silent cloud fallback; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: all supported local/remote combinations parse and validate; unsupported local pair errors unless explicit cloud fallback configured; no network call occurs without explicit consent/config; when fallback is explicitly enabled a user-visible log line or metric records the fallback for privacy audit. Outputs: backend matrix for STT/MT/TTS; config schema decision; fallback and consent contract; contract tests. Out of scope: implementing every remote provider (contract + minimal tests only).

evidence.source: Issue #457 body — Inputs cite src/providers/mod.rs, Google/local providers, config provider fields, fallback policies, privacy requirements

rows.title: MODEL-02 — macOS local model runtime validation and packaging constraints

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #458; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) benchmark/evidence artifact exists; (2) packaging plan excludes large model binaries unless explicitly approved; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: local-stt builds on macos-13/14; local-mt builds on macos-13/14; CPU fallback runs without GPU/Metal; model cache uses `~/Library/Application Support/tui-translator/models` or documented ProjectDirs equivalent; RSS/CPU thresholds recorded against baseline. Outputs: macOS build/runtime validation report; model cache path validation; Metal/CoreML decision or deferral; packaging constraints for models and native libraries. Depends on MODEL-01, CI-01.

evidence.source: Issue #458 body — Inputs cite local-stt/local-mt Cargo features, model cache code, offline benchmark evidence

rows.title: QA-01 — ISO 25010 + ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 QA plan and traceability matrix

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #459; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) QA plan covers functional suitability, reliability, performance efficiency, compatibility, security, maintainability, portability, and interaction capability (ISO/IEC 25010); (2) traceability matrix complete for P0 scope; (3) Opus review confirms standards alignment. Test cases: every P0 requirement maps to ≥ 1 test or evidence item; every WBS implementation issue references ≥ 1 test ID; CI/doc lint fails for missing referenced test IDs once tooling exists; Opus QA review validates standards mapping. Outputs: Master Test Plan deliverable; traceability matrix; Test-ID naming convention; quality threshold table. Out of scope: purchasing copyrighted standards text (use compliant summaries).

evidence.source: Issue #459 body — Inputs cite docs/04-verification-plan.md, ISO/IEC 25010, ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119; Acceptance + Test cases sections

rows.title: TEST-01 — Deterministic simulation harness for audio, provider, network, PTY, and virtual mic

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #460; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) simulation evidence artifacts generated in a stable schema; (2) L1-L4 can run without a live meeting except explicitly marked hardware tests; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: audio fixture replayer drives pipeline headlessly; provider mock returns 429/503/slow responses with retry/status verification; PTY resize/cleanup tests pass; virtual mic memory PCM roundtrip validates RMS/drop/latency gates; soak file-source mode emits schema-versioned JSON. Outputs: simulation harness plan; fixture replayer tasks; fake provider/network chaos tasks; virtual mic memory-tier tests; evidence schema. Depends on QA-01. Out of scope: replacing L5 human acceptance.

evidence.source: Issue #460 body — Inputs cite tests/fixtures/, run_soak, vbcable_ci_probe, PTY harness, contract mocks

rows.title: CI-01 — CI matrix expansion for Windows/macOS/features and required gates

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #461; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) CI run URL shows required contexts green; (2) branch protection can use the new required checks; (3) no existing Windows gate is weakened; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: tampering Cargo.lock causes --locked jobs to fail; required feature combinations compile on macOS and Windows; beta toolchain failure is allowed-fail only where documented; VMIC hardware-dependent jobs remain skip-safe with explicit evidence. Outputs: Windows + macos-13 + macos-14 CI matrix; MSRV/stable/beta policy; feature matrix; required status check list. Depends on QA-01, TEST-01. Out of scope: signing/notarization, SBOM (SEC-01/REL-01).

evidence.source: Issue #461 body — Inputs cite .github/workflows/ci.yml, Cargo MSRV 1.86, Cargo features, existing VMIC/soak jobs

rows.title: SEC-01 — Supply-chain/security gates, SBOM, SLSA, and signing policy

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #462; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) security gate required before release; (2) no secrets or private paths leak into CI artifacts/logs; (3) SBOM and provenance generated for release assets; (4) tui-security-auditor or Opus security review clean. Test cases: fake secret fixture blocks PR without exposing real credentials; cargo deny failure blocks PR; SBOM validates and is attached to release artifacts; cosign verify succeeds for untampered artifacts and fails after byte tamper; logs/telemetry do not expose Google API keys, sensitive BlackHole device names, or user home paths. Outputs: cargo-deny/audit gate; secret scanning gate; SAST/SARIF plan; CycloneDX/SPDX SBOM; SLSA provenance; cosign/signing policy. Depends on CI-01. Out of scope: procurement of code-signing certificates (REL-01).

evidence.source: Issue #462 body — Inputs cite deny.toml, .github/workflows/release.yml, PRIVACY.md, config/log surfaces, GitHub OIDC, SBOM/SLSA tooling

rows.title: REL-01 — Cross-platform release packaging, notarization, and GA promotion plan

before.acceptance: Implementation matches issue body acceptance criteria for #463; evidence linked in issue; manual review optional.

after.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) release checklist includes all CI, security, QA, soak, human acceptance, and Opus review gates; (2) artifacts are signed/notarized where applicable; (3) GA promotion is blocked until all gates pass; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: lipo -info or equivalent confirms universal/target architecture policy; codesign --verify passes; notarytool submit --wait returns accepted; stapler and spctl verification pass; Gatekeeper first-launch UX validated on a clean VM; rollback drill proves users can return to previous release. Outputs: v2 alpha/beta/GA release plan; macOS packaging workflow; notarization and stapling verification; rollback plan; release-readiness checklist. Depends on CI-01, SEC-01, QA-01, TEST-01, MACOS-02, MACOS-04. Out of scope: Mac App Store distribution, Homebrew tap.

evidence.source: Issue #463 body — Inputs cite .github/workflows/release.yml, packaging/windows/, Apple Developer ID requirements; Acceptance + Test cases sections; issue has 1 prior comment

remaining_human_clarification_blockers.blocker: Operational/recruiting task — requires human to identify and confirm at least two named reviewers (one bilingual JA/VI, one non-developer), schedule live Zoom session(s) on real hardware, and produce verification-evidence/<rc-tag>/acceptance-log.md. The issue body itself states automation cannot complete it.

notes: No issue bodies, comments, labels, or GitHub state were modified. Only read operations (gh api GET) were used.
src/** was NOT modified. Per-task scope is verification-evidence/w0-r4/** and the board snapshot only.

files_touched_hint values are predictions for downstream implementers; they include paths verified to exist in the repo today plus the new files (clearly named or marked new) those issues will create.

Confidence promoted to MEDIUM (not HIGH) because each issue still requires Opus/specialist review before close (per the 'Opus review gate' section in every body) and several depend on a spike (MACOS-01) or a foundational decision (MODEL-01, QA-01, CI-01) that has not yet landed.
